perturbation.original_text: Marketing Affiliate shall be responsible for collecting all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall bear sole responsibility for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as compensation for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto.
perturbation.changed_text: Marketing Affiliate shall use commercially reasonable efforts to collect all amounts due directly from the Consumer and shall share responsibility with Equidata for non-payment of any fees charged to the Consumer. Marketing Affiliate shall pay to Equidata, as compensation for its providing of Services under this agreement, such amounts as outlined and detailed in Exhibit A attached hereto. However, the final amount is subject to consumer payment rates.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding the Marketing Affiliate's financial responsibilities. The original text clearly states the Marketing Affiliate is 'solely responsible' for non-payment, while the modified version states they 'share responsibility', and 'subject to consumer payment rates'. This contradicts the clause in Disputes, Section 2: 'Such arbitration does not relieve Marketing Affiliate from its obligation to promptly pay for undisputed charges in accordance with the terms of this Agreement'. The change makes it unclear if Marketing Affiliate is fully liable for consumer non-payment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: In the case of disputed charge, defined as a non-payment of an invoice for which notice of dispute has been given in writing by Marketing Affiliate to Equidata, Equidata or Marketing Affiliate may choose arbitration and Marketing Affiliate and Equidata shall be obligated by the terms agreed upon by arbitration and all monies determined owed shall be considered due and payable immediately. Such arbitration does not relieve Marketing Affiliate from its obligation to promptly pay for undisputed charges in accordance with the terms of this Agreement. Such disputes shall be settled by arbitration in the City of Newport News, Virginia. Marketing Affiliate shall give Equidata written demand of dispute within 10 days of the due date of the invoice. The demand shall set forth a statement for the nature of the dispute and the amount involved. If Equidata and Marketing Affiliate can not resolve the dispute on their own within 10 days after Equidata receive said dispute, the parties shall jointly select an arbitrator.
perturbation.justification: The original text clearly assigns 'sole responsibility' for non-payment to the Marketing Affiliate. The changed text introduces ambiguity by stating they 'share responsibility' and payment is 'subject to consumer payment rates'. This directly contradicts the clause that 'Such arbitration does not relieve Marketing Affiliate from its obligation to promptly pay for undisputed charges'. The modified text creates uncertainty about the Marketing Affiliate's financial obligations and whether they are fully liable for consumer non-payment, making these sections incompatible and leading to a strong in-text contradiction. This falls under the Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction perturbation type.

perturbation.original_text: Marketing Affiliate shall give Equidata written demand of dispute within 10 days of the due date of the invoice. The demand shall set forth a statement for the nature of the dispute and the amount involved. If Equidata and Marketing Affiliate can not resolve the dispute on their own within 10 days after Equidata receive said dispute, the parties shall jointly select an arbitrator.
perturbation.changed_text: Marketing Affiliate shall give Equidata written demand of dispute within 30 days of the due date of the invoice. The demand shall set forth a general statement of the dispute and a rough estimate of the amount involved. If Equidata and Marketing Affiliate cannot resolve the dispute within 3 business days after Equidata receives said dispute, either party may select an arbitrator.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces a contradiction by altering the dispute resolution timeline and requirements. The original text specifies a 10-day window for written demand and a 10-day resolution period before arbitration. The revised text extends the demand window to 30 days, shortens the resolution period to 3 business days, and modifies the demand requirements to include only a 'general statement' and 'rough estimate'. This contradicts Section 7.2, which states: 'This Agreement may be terminated by either party with cause upon thirty (30) days written notice. Upon Marketing Affiliate's default in payment or other breach of this Agreement, Equidata may terminate this Agreement without notice to Marketing Affiliate.' The shorter dispute resolution period introduced may not allow enough time to properly investigate any disputes before a potential default and termination occur, thus creating uncertainty. Moreover, the specific timelines for arbitration commencement described in the same section may not be achievable within this shorter time frame.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement may be terminated by either party with cause upon thirty (30) days written notice. Upon Marketing Affiliate's default in payment or other breach of this Agreement, Equidata may terminate this Agreement without notice to Marketing Affiliate. Upon termination for any reason, Equidata reserves the right to deactivate Marketing Affiliate's access to the services including the Equidata Web Site. Termination does not release Marketing Affiliate from paying all amounts owed to Equidata.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the dispute resolution timeline from 10 days to 3 business days and extends the demand window to 30 days. This directly contradicts the contradicted text, which allows Equidata to terminate the agreement without notice upon Marketing Affiliate's breach. The shortened dispute resolution period might not allow sufficient time to resolve disputes before termination, creating an inconsistency and potential for unfair termination. Additionally, the change reduces the requirement from a 'statement for the nature of the dispute' to a 'general statement', further contradicting the level of detail implied in the termination clause of 'default in payment or other breach'.

perturbation.original_text: Marketing Affiliate shall not directly or indirectly solicit an existing business customer of Equidata during the term and condition of this Agreement other than for joint marketing purposes. Further, Marketing Affiliate shall not market similar products from competing companies on any Web Site Landing Page containing the Equidata or Marketing Affiliate Web link as long as this Agreement is in effect.
perturbation.changed_text: Marketing Affiliate may directly or indirectly solicit an existing business customer of Equidata during the term and condition of this Agreement only with prior written consent from Equidata. Further, Marketing Affiliate may market similar products from competing companies on any Web Site Landing Page containing the Equidata or Marketing Affiliate Web link with notice to Equidata as long as this Agreement is in effect.
perturbation.explanation: This change introduces ambiguity regarding Marketing Affiliate's ability to market competing services and solicit Equidata's clients. The original clause prohibited direct or indirect solicitation and marketing of similar products. The modified clause permits these activities with 'prior written consent' and 'with notice', respectively. This conflicts with Section 8.1 which states: 'Marketing Affiliate does not engage in any business with respect to, and the Marketing Affiliate Web Site will not be used, or display any materials, in any form or medium, in connection with a credit clinic, credit repair or restoration, credit counseling firm, financial counseling firm, detective agency, private investigation, security services, practice of law, news reporting or journalism, or fraudulent or unethical conduct.' It becomes unclear if the Marketing Affiliate is permitted to promote competing services that could overlap with these restricted businesses given the new permissions and prior restrictions, creating uncertainty for enforcement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Marketing Affiliate does not engage in any business with respect to, and the Marketing Affiliate Web Site will not be used, or display any materials, in any form or medium, in connection with a credit clinic, credit repair or restoration, credit counseling firm, financial counseling firm, detective agency, private investigation, security services, practice of law, news reporting or journalism, or fraudulent or unethical conduct.
perturbation.justification: The modified text allows the Marketing Affiliate to solicit Equidata's customers and market competing products with consent/notice, while the contradicted text prohibits the Marketing Affiliate from engaging in businesses (or displaying materials) related to certain restricted categories like credit repair, legal services, or unethical conduct. This creates an ambiguity because the new permission to solicit and market could easily involve promoting services that fall within the prohibited categories, creating a direct conflict. Therefore, this introduces ambiguity regarding the Marketing Affiliate's obligations and is therefore a strong in-text contradiction.
